charter: Continue governed execution until the goals in .planning/VISION.md are materially satisfied with real product and operational outcomes, not only internally consistent artifacts. Treat deployment, release execution, and runnable/live surfaces as in scope whenever they are the shortest path to making tusq.dev real. PM must continuously add roadmap and system-spec milestones for product, infrastructure, deployment, release execution, and operational readiness without waiting for human prompts. Prefer existing infrastructure already discoverable in the repo or environment; if GCP or GCS deployment context already exists, use it rather than inventing a new hosting target. Use human escalation only when a truly external decision, credentialed action, or irreversible operator action is required.
acceptance_contract: PM adds operational roadmap items when needed without human prompting
Existing discoverable deployment infrastructure is preferred over inventing a new host
Human escalation is used only for real external decisions or actions
The run does not stop at docs or launch artifacts when a real deployable outcome is still missing

history.reason: planning artifacts for this intent already exist on disk; intent superseded during approval
